description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular agricultural building with a steep gambrel roof covered in reddish-brown tiles, providing ample overhead space. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal wooden planks, with a brick foundation visible at the base. One side features a large, double wooden barn door with black metal hinges, while the opposite side has a single wooden door and three rectangular windows, allowing natural light inside. The roof overhangs the walls, offering additional protection from the elements. The structure is robust and utilitarian, designed for livestock shelter and storage. The overall appearance is functional, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing practicality and durability.
description: A sturdy wooden cowshed with a gambrel tile roof, large barn doors, and side windows, set on a brick foundation. The design is practical and traditional, suited for agricultural use.
description_view_0: The view shows the broad gambrel roof and the main double barn doors, one of which is open, revealing the entrance.
description_view_1: This end view highlights the steep roof pitch and plain wooden wall, with no visible doors or windows.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the building, showing the full expanse of the roof and a solid wooden wall with no openings.
description_view_3: This end view displays a single wooden door and three rectangular windows, providing access and light to the interior.
attribute_door_style: double wooden barn door with black metal strap hinges; single wooden plank door on side
attribute_window_presence: three rectangular windows on the side
